limitations: When the set of monitors or the measurement setup changes, the networks must be retrained; the computed sub-band significance is affected by the emanations of the other monitors present in the experiment as well as by the measurement setup used (the antennas did not capture emanations below 90 MHz).
source_file: old/217 - AI-Assisted Identification of State and Type of Flat-Panel Monitors in the Presence of EM Noise.pdf

title: Enhancing Information Extraction in EMC Measurements through Artificial Intelligence
ai_technique: Convolutional autoencoder for near-field data dimensionality reduction + surrogate regression model
core_claim: The work describes an ML model of the relationship between PCB design parameters and EMC properties (far-field characteristics) derived from near-field scans, where convolutional autoencoders reduce the high dimensionality of near-field data to a low-dimensional latent space for identifying sources of EMC problems.
results: The work is predominantly methodological/conceptual - it describes the proposed procedure (data augmentation, combination of simulation and measurement, autoencoder training, use as a surrogate model for optimization) without providing specific numerical accuracy metrics.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: quantitative validation of the accuracy of the proposed approach on test data and a discussion of robustness to noise in real measurements are missing.
source_file: old/87 - Enhancing Information Extraction in EMC Measurements through Artificial Intelligence.pdf

title: Electromagnetic interference elimination via active sensing and deep learning prediction for radiofrequency shielding-free MRI
ai_technique: CNN for predicting EMI from reference sensing coils (extended study relative to the conference paper)
core_claim: Extended version of a previous study: a method of active EMI sensing and deep learning (CNN) predicts and removes EMI components from the received MRI signal, thereby enabling MRI operation without RF shielding even on a conventional superconducting scanner.
results: On an unshielded 0.055 T scanner, the method achieved an image SNR comparable to a shielded room and outperformed existing analytical methods (spectral transfer function, EDITER); a preliminary experiment demonstrated functionality also on a 1.5 T scanner with incomplete shielding.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the available text does not include a discussion/limitations passage (it consists only of the introduction and reference list); a likely limitation is the need to train a specific CNN model for each scan and dependence on the number and placement of the sensing coils.
source_file: old/241 - Electromagnetic interference elimination via active sensing and deep learning prediction for radiofrequency shielding-free MRI.pdf

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Electromagnetic Compatibility Test Signal Prediction Using LSTM Networks
ai_technique: LSTM (conceptual framework, without implementation)
core_claim: The author proposes a conceptual methodological framework using LSTM networks to predict the time-domain characteristics of EMC test signals (transient pulses, modulated sinusoids) in order to speed up and streamline the EMC testing process.
results: This is a purely conceptual/methodological article without experimental implementation or specific numerical results; only the proposed procedure for data collection, preprocessing, LSTM architecture, and evaluation is described.
limitations: The author explicitly states that this is a conceptual proposal requiring future implementation and validation on real data, and names open questions regarding model generalization, computational complexity, and prediction uncertainty quantification.
source_file: old/81 - A Deep Learning Approach to Electromagnetic Compatibility Test Signal Prediction Using LSTM Networks.pdf

title: Adaptive On-the-Fly Scan Method for Fast and Efficient Planar Near-Field Acquisition
ai_technique: Bayesian optimization with a GPR surrogate
core_claim: Proposes adaptive planar near-field scanning that replaces point-by-point sampling with iterative on-the-fly line scans; the number and placement of lines are dynamically chosen by Bayesian optimization with a GPR surrogate model of the 1-D field aggregate.
results: Both numerical and experimental validation (wire and patch antenna structures): a complete adaptive OTF scan in under 1 minute (about 25 line scans, 1-2 s per line at speeds up to 2 m/s); a GPR-BO iteration takes less than 0.1 s; initialization requires only 4-6 uniform lines.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method is demonstrated at individual frequencies; the authors leave extension to broadband scenarios and fast far-field prediction for future research.
source_file: old/278 - Adaptive On-the-Fly Scan Method for Fast and Efficient Planar Near-Field Acquisition.pdf

title: Evaluation of Neural Networks to Predict Target Impedance Violations of Power Delivery Networks
ai_technique: ANN classifier with input preprocessing (ring/rectangular sector method)
core_claim: The authors present an ANN approach for predicting whether a PDN configuration with randomly placed decoupling capacitors violates the target impedance, showing the effect of the input data preprocessing method (direct method vs. circular/rectangular sectors) on accuracy.
results: The circular sector method achieves the best accuracy of 88%, compared to 86% for rectangular sectors and 75% for the direct method; accuracy remains above 85% even with target impedance variation of -6 to +16 dB, and the network retains usability even when the IC port position shifts, without requiring retraining.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the network was trained and tested on a single board topology with a fixed range of decoupling capacitor parameters; robustness against fundamentally different PDN topologies was not verified.
source_file: old/304 - Evaluation of Neural Networks to Predict Target Impedance Violations of Power Delivery Networks.pdf

title: ANN Performance for the Prediction of High-Speed Digital Interconnects over Multiple PCBs
ai_technique: ANN (feedforward regression) for S-parameter and FOM prediction
core_claim: The authors show that an ANN trained on data from physics-based modeling (PBV) can predict the behavior of complex high-speed interconnects (transmission, FEXT, and weighted power sums) across multiple printed circuit boards up to 100 GHz.
results: The test RMSE reaches 0.0258 (transmission) and 0.0043 (FEXT) for S-parameter prediction; for FOM prediction, the test RMSE values are 10.3 (WPSXT), 194.3 (WPT), and 17.5 (WSXTR); ANNs found via Bayesian optimization do not achieve substantially better results than manual grid search.
limitations: The authors state that the predictions are not guaranteed to be passive, some datasets exhibit unexpected S-parameter behavior leading to poor predictions, and only magnitude, not phase, is predicted.
source_file: old/301 - ANN Performance for the Prediction of High-Speed Digital Interconnects over Multiple PCBs.pdf

title: Bayesian Optimization for Signal Transmission Including Crosstalk in a Via Array
ai_technique: Bayesian optimization (GP with Matérn 5/2 kernel) compared with a genetic algorithm
core_claim: The authors show that Bayesian optimization can be used to optimize printed circuit board interconnects (vias, via arrays) in terms of transmission and crosstalk, with results compared against a genetic algorithm and physically derived design rules.
results: For a single via, the BO result is comparable to GA; at 15 Gbps, BO achieves a higher result than GA, whereas at 25 Gbps, GA performs better; for via arrays, BO shows a greater dependence on hyperparameters, but the predicted parameters correlate with the results of previous studies.
limitations: The authors state that for the more complex via array model, there is a greater dependence of the results on the choice of Bayesian optimization hyperparameters, indicating the method's sensitivity for more complex problems.
source_file: old/302 - Bayesian Optimization for Signal Transmission Including Crosstalk in a Via Array.pdf

title: Comparison of Collaborative versus Extended Artificial Neural Networks for PDN Design
ai_technique: Collaborative vs. extended ANN (feedforward, backpropagation)
core_claim: The authors compare two approaches for extending ANNs to predict PDN target impedance violations beyond the decoupling capacitor distribution — a collaborative ensemble of multiple ANNs versus a single extended ANN with an additional input parameter — and show that both can compensate for the accuracy loss in the extended design space.
results: The extended ANN achieves comparable accuracy (80-90%) to the collaborative approach but requires 2 to 2.5 times fewer training samples; for target impedance variation, the extended ANN achieves over 90% accuracy with 5% of the training data.
limitations: The authors state that the need to use the collaborative versus extended approach depends strongly on the specific design space and the effect of the given variation on the PDN, meaning the generalization is not universal.
source_file: old/303 - Comparison of Collaborative versus Extended Artificial Neural Networks for PDN Design.pdf

title: Demystifying Machine Learning for Signal and Power Integrity Problems in Packaging
ai_technique: Survey of ANN/DNN and Bayesian learning (GP, BO) for SI/PI modeling and optimization
core_claim: A survey/tutorial article explaining the fundamentals of neural networks and Bayesian learning (Gaussian processes, Bayesian optimization) for signal and power integrity (SI/PI) problems in packaging, with an emphasis on intuition and a summary of recent developments and future research directions.
results: As a survey paper, it does not present its own quantitative experiments but summarizes results from cited works (BO finding optimal stack-up designs, ANN models with high accuracy using fewer samples) and provides links to open-source software.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: this is a survey/tutorial article without its own experimental validation; its scope is determined by a subjective selection of topics cited as current as of 2020.
source_file: old/305 - Demystifying Machine Learning for Signal and Power Integrity Problems in Packaging.pdf

title: Worst-Case Eye Analysis of High-Speed Channels Based on Bayesian Optimization
ai_technique: Bayesian optimization for worst-case eye-diagram estimation (Gray coding)
core_claim: The authors propose an optimization approach (Worst-eye) using Bayesian optimization and Gray-code-based mapping to quickly find the worst-case eye-diagram characteristics of high-speed channels, including intersymbol interference and crosstalk, without requiring a lengthy transient simulation.
results: The proposed method achieves a speedup of up to 47x in determining eye width and height and up to 23x in determining the overall worst-case eye opening, compared to transient simulation, while maintaining accuracy.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method was validated on a limited number of examples (a SerDes channel on a PCB and a channel in a system-on-package); generalization to other channel types and noise mechanisms was not systematically verified.
source_file: old/306 - Worst-Case Eye Analysis of High-Speed Channels Based on Bayesian Optimization.pdf

title: Evaluation of Support Vector Machines for PCB based Power Delivery Network Classification
ai_technique: SVM (classification, ring-sector preprocessing)
core_claim: SVMs can classify whether a PDN on a PCB with a given decoupling capacitor distribution meets the target impedance; preprocessing of input features (ring sectors around IC vias) and adding geometric features fundamentally affect prediction accuracy.
results: The best accuracy is 91.6% (linear spacing, 4 sectors); 90% is already achieved with 14% of the 18,000 samples; the extended SVM with cavity height as an additional feature maintains over 80% accuracy across geometries and performs comparably to an ANN.
limitations: Prediction accuracy saturates below 92% regardless of the amount of training data and depends strongly on the chosen preprocessing; Not stated explicitly: the data originate from a physics-based model of a simple two-layer structure.
source_file: old/287 - Evaluation of Support Vector Machines for PCB based Power Delivery Network Classification.pdf

title: SI/PI-Database of PCB-Based Interconnects for Machine Learning Applications
ai_technique: ANN for S-parameter prediction and structure classification (demonstration use cases on the database)
core_claim: The authors present a publicly available database of 78,961 variants of PCB interconnects (SI/PI/EMC) with data from physics-based simulations, intended for training and testing ML techniques, and demonstrate its use with ANN models.
results: The database contains 78,961 samples of various types of PCB interconnects; in example use cases, ANN models predict S-parameters and classify structures with good agreement against reference physical simulations.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the database covers only limited types of PCB structures generated by a single physics-based tool; generalization to other technologies and PCB layers is not guaranteed.
source_file: old/61 - SIPI-Database of PCB-Based Interconnects for Machine Learning Applications.pdf

title: ANN Hyperparameter Optimization by Genetic Algorithms for Via Interconnect Classification
ai_technique: Genetic algorithm for hyperparameter optimization of an ANN classifier
core_claim: The authors propose using a genetic algorithm for automatic hyperparameter selection of ANN classifiers for imbalanced classification tasks of via interconnects (impedance deviations, crosstalk), combined with class-balancing techniques (ROS, weighted cross-entropy).
results: The achieved classification accuracy exceeds 97% and the F1 score exceeds 0.88 for both tested tasks (via transition and a 5x5 via array); using weighted cross-entropy improves the F1 score to approximately 0.91-0.92, compared to 0.89 without balancing techniques.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: GA optimization requires non-trivial computation time (35-80 minutes on a standard PC), and the method was tested only on two specific types of via structures.
source_file: old/299 - ANN Hyperparameter Optimization by Genetic Algorithms for Via Interconnect Classification.pdf

title: Computational Intelligence Based Selection and Placement of Decoupling Capacitors: A Comparative Study
ai_technique: Metaheuristics: SA, PSO, GA, DE, TLBO, CSO, WO
core_claim: The authors propose a general computational-intelligence-based framework (metaheuristic algorithms) for the optimal selection and placement of decoupling capacitors in a power delivery network (PDN), aiming to minimize impedance below a target value.
results: A comparative study of seven metaheuristic algorithms (SA, PSO, GA, DE, TLBO, CSO, WO) on a real industrial PDN demonstrated that these methods can effectively reduce the network's self-impedance below the target impedance over the frequency range studied.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the comparison was performed on a single industrial PDN case; scalability and computational cost with a substantially larger number of capacitors/ports were not discussed in detail.
source_file: old/51 - Computational Intelligence Based Selection and Placement of Decoupling Capacitors.pdf

title: Knowledge-Based Neural Networks for Fast Design Space Exploration of Hybrid Copper-Graphene On-Chip Interconnect Networks
ai_technique: Knowledge-based artificial neural network (KBNN)
core_claim: The authors propose a knowledge-based neural network (KBNN) combining empirical models with full-wave EM simulation to predict the per-unit-length (p.u.l.) parameters (resistance, inductance, capacitance, conductance) of hybrid copper-graphene on-chip interconnects from their geometry, intended for fast SPICE modeling of signal integrity and transient analysis.
results: The KBNN achieves accuracy comparable to a conventional ANN but at significantly lower training cost (fewer required EM simulations), enabling faster exploration of the interconnect design space.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the available text does not include a discussion of limitations; the model's generality is presumably limited to the tested technology and geometry of copper-graphene interconnects, and retraining would be required for different material/structural configurations.
source_file: old/186 - Knowledge-Based Neural Networks for Fast Design Space Exploration of Hybrid Copper-Graphene On-Chip Interconnect Networks.pdf

title: Parametric S-Parameters for PCB based Power Delivery Network Design Using Machine Learning
ai_technique: ANN (feed-forward)
core_claim: A methodology using an ANN trained on 10,000 physics-based simulations of parametric S-parameters enables answering PDN design questions (board resonance, target impedance violations), with the data made available in the SI/PI-Database.
results: The ANN predicts board resonance with a mean absolute error of 3.42 MHz and classifies target impedance violations with 97.1% accuracy (51,490 terminated PDNs); the PB model was validated against a FEM solver (30 s vs. 5400 s).
limitations: The authors discuss limitations of data generation: full exploration of the space (over 10^6 combinations) would require hundreds of days of computation and approximately 16.6 TB of storage, hence a sparse sampling strategy and identification of the required sampling density are necessary.
source_file: old/286 - Parametric S-Parameters for PCB based Power Delivery Network Design Using Machine Learning.pdf

title: Fast PDN Impedance Prediction Using Deep Learning
ai_technique: DNN (training data from BEM)
core_claim: Introduces a concept for predicting the PDN impedance curve for an arbitrary board shape, stackup, IC position, and decap placement using a deep neural network trained on more than one million boards generated by a fast boundary element method (BEM).
results: The trained DNN predicts impedance in about 0.1 s -- more than 100 times faster than BEM and 5000 times faster than full-wave simulation -- and generalizes to new board configurations; the BEM reference agrees with HFSS (5 s vs. over 5 minutes).
limitations: Not stated explicitly: a quasi-static BEM model and demonstration in the low-frequency band (up to about 20 MHz); the DNN's accuracy relative to the reference is described only as tolerable and depends on synthetic, randomly generated boards.
source_file: old/279 - Fast PDN Impedance Prediction Using Deep Learning.pdf

title: Bayesian Optimization of First-Order Continuous-Time Linear Equalization in High-Speed Links Including Crosstalk
ai_technique: Bayesian optimization (GP surrogate, Noisy Expected Improvement)
core_claim: The authors propose using Bayesian optimization with a Gaussian process to find optimal CTLE equalizer coefficients for high-speed links, newly including the effect of far-end crosstalk (FEXT) as noise in the model.
results: BO finds the optimal CTLE coefficients within 15-30 iterations, compared to 12,000 combinations for grid search; the achieved FoM values are comparable to or better than grid search (17.92 vs. 17.86) and better than the analytical pulse-shaping method (14.4).
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method was verified only on two specific channels with a homoscedastic crosstalk noise model, which may not correspond to more general scenarios.
source_file: old/295 - Bayesian Optimization of First-Order Continuous-Time Linear Equalization in High-Speed Links Including Crosstalk.pdf

title: AI-Enhanced Modal Decomposition Method for Fast and Efficient PCB Modeling and Signal Integrity
ai_technique: AI-based pattern recognition + hybrid analytical-numerical-AI modal decomposition
core_claim: An AI-enhanced modal decomposition method splits high-speed multilayer PCB interconnects into independent modal cells and combines analytical, numerical, and AI methods for their efficient evaluation and signal integrity prediction.
results: On 20 real PCB blocks, the method achieved an average frequency error of 2.3%/1.4% and an amplitude error of 0.3/0.6 dB for the 0-28/28-40 GHz bands compared to measurements.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the model was trained on only 3 of the 20 PCB blocks and validation was performed on a limited set of structures; general robustness for significantly different topologies is not demonstrated.
source_file: old/63 - AI-Enhanced Modal Decomposition Method for Fast and Efficient PCB Modeling and Signal Integrity.pdf

title: Modeling Electrically Long Interconnects Using Physics-Informed Delayed Gaussian Processes
ai_technique: GPR with a physics-informed periodic kernel (τGP), Gabor-based delay estimation
core_claim: A new delayed Gaussian process (τGP) with a physics-informed periodic kernel, whose fundamental frequencies correspond to tunable propagation delays, enables modeling of wideband, strongly oscillating S-parameters of electrically long interconnects, which standard smooth GP kernels fail to capture.
results: On two application examples, τGP outperforms standard GP, vector fitting, and delayed vector fitting, particularly at high frequencies where the response deviates from a rational approximation; the delays are automatically estimated using a Gabor transform combined with MMLE.
limitations: The authors state that further effort is needed to extend the model with additional design variables and to enforce physical properties (causality, passivity) on the predictions.
source_file: old/289 - Modeling Electrically Long Interconnects Using Physics-Informed Delayed Gaussian Processes.pdf

title: Modeling S-parameters of Interconnects using Periodic Gaussian Process Kernels
ai_technique: Gaussian process regression (GPR/kriging) with a new periodic kernel
core_claim: The authors propose a new periodic kernel for Gaussian processes that enables accurate modeling of wideband S-parameters of high-speed interconnects exhibiting oscillatory behavior caused by resonances and crosstalk.
results: The new kernel achieves substantially lower prediction error than standard kernels: average error -59 dB (vs. -16 dB for the SE kernel and -15 dB for the Matérn kernel), maximum error -38.7 dB (vs. -11 and -10.7 dB).
limitations: The authors state that kernel performance depends strongly on correctly estimating the number of periods: underestimation leads to underfitting, while overestimation leads to numerical instability.
source_file: old/296 - Modeling S-parameters of Interconnects using Periodic Gaussian Process Kernels.pdf

title: Generating AI modules for decoupling capacitor placement using simulation
ai_technique: ANN + Bayesian hyperparameter optimization
core_claim: A data generation and ANN training process that accounts not only for decoupling capacitors but also for the multilayer stackup and PCB power plane size enables prediction of the PDN impedance profile; Bayesian optimization tunes the ANN hyperparameters for higher accuracy.
results: The predicted impedance agrees well with a commercial PI simulator (eCADSTAR); BO tuning reduced the MSE from 1.37 to 0.7 (case 1) and from 1.26 to 0.29 (case 2), with the ANN being substantially faster than commercial PDN software during optimization iterations.
limitations: The authors state that the ANN's accuracy does not fully match full-wave or other numerical PI simulators; transfer learning and physics-informed ML for PCB structure modifications are left for future work.
source_file: old/280 - Generating AI modules for decoupling capacitor placement using simulation.pdf

title: Machine Learning Based PCB/Package Stack-up Optimization for Signal Integrity
ai_technique: Bayesian optimization + ANN surrogate model (BO-ANN)
core_claim: The authors propose a BO-ANN algorithm combining Bayesian optimization with an ANN surrogate model for fast optimization of PCB/package stack-ups (impedance and attenuation), including an effective dielectric constant strategy for generalization to multilayer dielectric stack-ups.
results: After training the ANN on more than 100,000 samples, the relative prediction error is below 1% for impedance Z0 and below 5% for attenuation; the algorithm completes 100 optimization iterations in 40 seconds; the deviation of the optimized Z0 from the target is below 1-2% for practical applications, and the attenuation deviation is below 11%.
limitations: The trained ANN's prediction error carries over into the difference between the optimization result and verification with a 2D solver; the effective dielectric constant strategy is only a rough estimate, which introduces additional error for multilayer stack-ups.
source_file: old/297 - Machine Learning Based PCB--Package Stack-up Optimization for Signal Integrity.pdf

title: An Electromagnetic Perspective of Artificial Intelligence Neuromorphic Chips
ai_technique: Survey of ML/DNN methods (DNN, MLP, SVR-AS) and GA optimization for SI/EMI
core_claim: The survey article maps the electromagnetic problems (EMI and signal integrity) of neuromorphic AI chips and summarizes the use of machine learning methods for modeling and optimizing these phenomena in high-speed interconnects and heterogeneous integration.
results: The survey summarizes a number of works demonstrating the successful use of DNN, SVR, and genetic algorithms for predicting package EMI radiation and optimizing the signal integrity of high-speed channels; it is a review without its own new quantitative results.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: this is a survey article without its own experiment; its limitations are the limited depth of coverage of a rapidly evolving field and the lack of a unified evaluation methodology across the cited works.
source_file: old/39 - An Electromagnetic Perspective of Artificial Intelligence Neuromorphic Chips.pdf

title: Engineering-Informed Design Space Reduction for PCB-Based Power Delivery Networks
ai_technique: ANN + data-driven sensitivity analysis
core_claim: Engineering-informed reduction of the PCB PDN design space, combining data-driven parameter sensitivity analysis with physics-based stackup reduction, enables generating substantially fewer samples for training ML tools used in the PDN design process.
results: The sampling design space was reduced by three decades, from more than 10^6 to 10^3 points (a reduction of over 1000x); the trained ANNs predict key features of PDN EM behavior and target impedance violations (smallest errors for TI = 0.1 Ω); the data are available in the SI/PI-database.
limitations: The authors state that the general applicability of the reduction approach to other design problems has yet to be demonstrated; further reduction (sensitivity-based adaptive sampling, reduction of additional parameters) remains to be explored.
source_file: old/291 - Engineering-Informed Design Space Reduction for PCB Based Power Delivery Networks.pdf

title: FNNs models for Regression of S-Parameters in Multilayer Interconnects with Different Electrical Lengths
ai_technique: FNN + GA hyperparameter optimization
core_claim: Feedforward neural networks with hyperparameters optimized by a genetic algorithm can predict the full S-parameter response (transmission, reflections, crosstalk) of multilayer interconnects; the required model complexity increases with the maximum electrical length of the structure.
results: For shorter structures (le up to approximately 3.1λ), MSE below 3·10⁻⁴; the reliable prediction limit of le ≈ 4.6λ was extended, by enlarging the network (512 neurons per layer), up to 19.2λ (correct prediction up to 24.2 GHz), though the full 40 GHz band was not achieved.
limitations: The authors state that for long electrical lengths, predictions do not cover the entire band of interest even with a substantially enlarged network; they recommend exploring other architectures (CNN, RNN, LSTM) and surrogate models.
source_file: old/292 - FNNs Models for Regression of S-Parameters in Multilayer Interconnects with Different Electrical Lengths.pdf

title: AI-supported PDN Design for PCBs in Automotive Applications
ai_technique: DQN (RL) + CNN impedance prediction
core_claim: The prototype layout tool BerEDA with AI modules supports PDN design for automotive PCBs: a CNN provides real-time impedance prediction and a color-coded guide for decoupling capacitor placement, an RL agent automatically selects decoupling capacitor types and positions, all integrated with commercial tools (ZUKEN eCADSTAR, Altium Designer).
results: The DQN agent with the CNN network solved approximately 87% of the generated test tasks with a response time of 4-6 s; CNN impedance predictions sufficiently agree with the ZUKEN simulator used as ground truth; the reward converged after approximately 8000 training steps.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the agent prioritizes speed over optimality and fails to solve approximately 13% of cases; prediction accuracy is tied to the ZUKEN simulator as a reference rather than to measurements.
source_file: old/281 - AI-supported PDN Design for PCBs in Automotive Applications.pdf

title: Machine Learning Based Data Validation for Signal Integrity and Power Integrity Using Supervised Time Series Classification
ai_technique: Deep networks for time series classification (TSC)
core_claim: Proposes an ML alternative to the FSV method (IEEE Std 1597.1) for SI/PI data validation: time-series classifiers trained on expert-labeled data predict the degree of agreement between two curves and enable systematic automated validation of 1-D datasets.
results: The trained network outperforms individual human raters in predicting the average expert score with accuracy above 70%; the framework is supplemented with uncertainty quantification (discrete CDF) for fast validation of large datasets and flagging problematic port pairs.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method depends on subjectively labeled training data from experts and was validated only on frequency-domain SI/PI data; the authors defer extensive generation and testing on time-domain waveforms to future work.
source_file: old/275 - Machine Learning Based Data Validation for Signal Integrity and Power Integrity Using Supervised Time Series Classification.pdf

title: Application of Gaussian Process Regression for Data Efficient Prediction of PCB-based Power Delivery Network Impedance Features
ai_technique: GPR + active learning + transfer learning
core_claim: GPR incorporated into active learning and transfer learning loops enables data-efficient prediction of key PDN impedance features as a substitute for expensive EM simulations; reusing data from similar problems (SI/PI database) reduces the number of newly generated samples.
results: For a 4-layer PCB, a normalized RMSE of approximately 2-3% was achieved for resonant features (fr 2.04%, ΔZr 3.1%, Δfs 2.24%); data from a closer subspace speed up convergence; some features (fm 54%, ΔZm >100%) are predicted poorly.
limitations: The model predicts some key features (the frequency and height of the first local maximum) with low accuracy; the study is limited to a simple 4-layer board with four varied parameters.
source_file: old/285 - Application of Gaussian Process Regression for Data Efficient Prediction of PCB-Based Power Delivery Network Impedance Features.pdf

title: Generation and Application of a Very Large Dataset for Signal Integrity Via Array and Link Analysis
ai_technique: ANN + GPR over a large dataset
core_claim: Presents the largest SI-analysis dataset to date (approximately 15,000 variants of PCB material and geometry across three via-array-and-trace cases, up to 60 GHz), generated by a physics-based model, and demonstrates its usability for multi-purpose ML analyses without a fixed via pattern.
results: Over 15,000 PCB configurations (more than 450 GB of network parameters) were generated and made available in the SI/PI-Database; GP and ANN models predicting network parameter features are fitted to the data, and the dependence of PCB parameters on the PCI Gen 6 clock rate is analyzed.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the data originate exclusively from physics-based simulations (without measurements), and the random placement of signal, power, and ground vias does not correspond to common SI design practice for via arrays.
source_file: old/276 - Generation and Application of a Very Large Dataset for Signal Integrity Via Array and Link Analysis.pdf

title: AI Workbench - Conceptual Workflow to Develop AI Models for SI/PI-Applications in PCB Development
ai_technique: RL (Advantage Actor Critic/A2C) for decap placement + ANN for SI parameter prediction
core_claim: The article presents the concept of an "AI Workbench" - a systematic workflow for developing AI/ML models (ANN, RL) for SI/PI applications in PCB design, demonstrated on examples of SI parameter prediction and RL-agent-based optimization of decap placement for a PDN.
results: An RL agent (Advantage Actor Critic) found the optimal placement of three decaps (from a range of 2.2 nF to 100 nF at 16 positions) meeting the target PDN impedance, with the proposed solution remaining valid even under changes in board geometry and IC position.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: this is a conceptual workflow verified on only two limited example scenarios, without systematic comparison to other optimization approaches and without validation on larger-scale industrial designs.
source_file: old/84 - AI Workbench - Conceptual Workflow to Develop AI Models for SIPI-Applications in PCB Development.pdf

title: Dimensional Reduction by Auto-Encoders in Machine Learning Based Power Integrity Analysis
ai_technique: 1D convolutional autoencoder (AE) for dimensionality reduction + ANN surrogate model, SHAP for interpretability
core_claim: The authors propose a framework combining a one-dimensional convolutional autoencoder (1D-CAE) for dimensionality reduction of the printed circuit board (PCB) design space with a downstream artificial neural network (ANN) for fast prediction of port-to-port impedances in power delivery network (PDN) analysis, including interpretation of parameter influence using SHAP values.
results: The trained autoencoder achieved a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.0198 in reconstructing impedance responses, outperforming the reference vector fitting method, which had an MSE of 0.202; the downstream ANN operating on the latent space enables fast prediction of the Z11, Z12, and Z22 impedances for PDN optimization.
limitations: The authors report slight overfitting of the network on the training data relative to the validation data, and the need for more extensive hyperparameter tuning instead of manual setting.
source_file: old/198 - Dimensional Reduction by Auto-Encoders in Machine Learning Based Power Integrity Analysis.pdf

title: PCB based Power Delivery Network Analysis Using Transfer Learning and Artificial Neural Networks
ai_technique: ANN + transfer learning
core_claim: Transfer learning combined with an ANN enables pretraining the model on existing SI/PI database data and then predicting the board resonant frequency using a small number of samples from a new problem, thereby reducing the need to generate new data for PCB PI analysis.
results: Over 30,000 EM simulations of five PCB cases were evaluated; with fewer than 50 training samples for a new case, TL reduces the error by about 2x; lbfgs outperforms adam for small datasets, and limiting fine-tuning to 5 iterations (5-shot) prevents overfitting.
limitations: The authors observe overfitting with a very small number of samples and longer fine-tuning (100-shot); the benefit of transfer learning appears only when fewer than approximately 50 samples of the new problem are available.
source_file: old/284 - PCB Based Power Delivery Network Analysis Using Transfer Learning and Artificial Neural Networks.pdf

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Temperature-Dependent Dielectric and Conductive Parameters Based On Differential Stripline Characteristics
ai_technique: 1D-CNN
core_claim: A 1D-CNN trained on simulated modal propagation constants and characteristic impedances of a differential stripline (up to 25 GHz) can predict temperature-dependent parameters of the dielectric (wideband Debye) and conductor (cannonball Huray) material models for 25-125 °C.
results: Normalized RMSE below 5% for all three parameters (ε∞ 3.14%, Δε 2.17%, Rz 1.55%) on 1000 samples; validation outside the training space gives errors of 0.5-4%, except for very low roughness Rz < 1 µm (error approximately 40%).
limitations: The authors acknowledge a high Rz prediction error for very smooth copper foils (<1 µm) due to sparse training-data coverage of this region; however, the effect on the resulting attenuation is practically negligible.
source_file: old/283 - Data-Driven Prediction of Temperature-Dependent Dielectric and Conductive Parameters Based on Differential Stripline Characteristics.pdf

title: Unified Pre-Processing Steps Reducing the PCB Design Space to Enable ML Applications for Signal and Power Integrity Analysis
ai_technique: HDBSCAN clustering (+ Delaunay triangulation)
core_claim: Unified preprocessing steps and PCB design space analysis (splitting into SI and PI domains, removing redundancies, and normalizing the representation) enable reduction of the design space and standardization of data for ML applications in SI/PI.
results: Reduction of internal ground layers is valid for PI but fails for SI above 25 GHz; using PML conditions instead of PMC shortens the simulation from 7 h to 25 s; HDBSCAN clustering combined with Delaunay triangulation robustly identifies via arrays and their pitch even for irregular patterns.
limitations: The authors state that a significant portion of SI parameters (via constellations, via stubs, and pads) was not covered, and that design space reduction cannot be summarized into a generally applicable formula; further investigation is needed.
source_file: old/282 - Unified Pre-Processing Steps Reducing the PCB Design Space to Enable ML Applications for Signal and Power Integrity Analysis.pdf

title: Prediction of Metallic Conductor Voltage Owing to Electromagnetic Coupling Via a Hybrid ANFIS and Backtracking Search Algorithm
ai_technique: ANFIS + backtracking search algorithm (BSA)
core_claim: A hybrid ANFIS model, whose training is optimized by the backtracking search algorithm (BSA), can accurately and reliably predict the voltage induced by electromagnetic coupling from an HV line onto parallel metal pipelines (both mitigated and unmitigated), for the design of a mitigation system per IEEE Std 80.
results: Predictions were validated using datasets from the CDEGS software; the hybrid ANFIS-BSA achieves higher accuracy than ANN, SVR, and ANFIS optimized by other well-known optimization algorithms, under changes in system parameters.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: validation is performed only against CDEGS simulations without field measurements, and for specific configurations of the shared line-pipeline corridor.
source_file: old/28 - Prediction of Metallic Conductor Voltage Owing to Electromagnetic Coupling Via a Hybrid ANFIS and Backtracking Search Algorithm.pdf

title: Intelligent Electromagnetic Compatibility Management of Cell Phones by Using Knowledge Graphs
ai_technique: Knowledge graph (Neo4j) + LSTM + MLP + Word2vec
core_claim: The paper claims that combining a knowledge graph of interfering and sensitive components on a mobile phone PCB with machine learning methods (LSTM, MLP, Word2vec) enables, for the first time, the automated generation of EMC management reports from interactive query sessions.
results: Entity extraction accuracy reached 75.6% and the F1 score reached 70.9%; two validation experiments demonstrated the feasibility of the method for evaluating EMC coupling between components on the phone PCB (e.g., evaluating interference between traces using triple functions).
limitations: The authors state that the knowledge graph is currently limited to mobile phones and needs to be extended with additional entities, relationships, and EMC rules for other electronic products; for practical deployment, they are preparing automated entity extraction from CAD files.
source_file: old/258 - Intelligent Electromagnetic Compatibility Management of Cell Phones by Using Knowledge Graphs.pdf

title: Prediction of MRI RF Exposure for Implantable Plate Devices Using Artificial Neural Network
ai_technique: ANN with feature selection via the MIV method and optimization of initial weights using a genetic algorithm (GA)
core_claim: The authors propose a synthesizing framework combining an ANN with the mean impact value (MIV) algorithm for feature selection and a genetic algorithm (GA) for optimizing the network's initial weights and thresholds, for fast prediction of the peak 1g-averaged SAR of multi-configuration implantable plate devices in the MRI environment.
results: Compared to the unmodified ANN, the mean squared error (MSE) of the SAR1g prediction was reduced by 28.06% when using the MIV algorithm and by 55.29% for the complete synthesizing framework; the resulting MSE reached 8.16 W²/kg², and the correlation between the predicted and calculated SAR1g was 0.994 across 576 implant samples.
limitations: The authors state that the approach has so far been validated on simplified plate implants, and they plan to expand the number and types of training samples, extract more general features for various implants, and account for additional factors affecting RF heating (device position, orientation, RF coil type).
source_file: old/249 - Prediction of MRI RF Exposure for Implantable Plate Devices Using Artificial Neural Network.pdf

title: Electric and Magnetic Field Estimation Under Overhead Transmission Lines Using Artificial Neural Networks
ai_technique: Feedforward ANN (trained using the scaled conjugate gradient method)
core_claim: Two feedforward neural networks estimate the electric field intensity and magnetic flux density in the vicinity of high-voltage overhead power lines as a fast substitute for analytical methods (CSM, Biot-Savart law) for exposure assessment.
results: The ANN estimates closely match the results of the charge simulation method (CSM) and the Biot-Savart method, as well as real measurements on two 400 kV lines, for various line configurations, voltages, and currents.
limitations: The networks are trained for fixed voltage and current values; other values require additional output scaling. Validation is limited to specific line configurations and two real measurements.
source_file: old/223 - Electric and Magnetic Field Estimation Under Overhead Transmission Lines Using Artificial Neural Networks.pdf

title: Intelligent Electromagnetic Compatibility Diagnosis and Management With Collective Knowledge Graphs and Machine Learning
ai_technique: LSTM for entity extraction from text + decision trees for multi-entity extraction + knowledge graph
core_claim: The authors propose a method for intelligent EMC diagnostics and management based on a "collective knowledge graph" (rule/maxterm/basic unit/entity layers) combined with LSTM for entity extraction from interactive user queries, enabling rapid EMC analysis on the order of seconds instead of traditional simulation.
results: The method improved computational efficiency by 3x, reduced relationship storage requirements by 76% and node-attribute storage by 60.7%, increased entity identification accuracy from 77.7% to 99.5%, and reduced the number of interactive query rounds needed from six to four (a 33.3% improvement) compared to a fully connected knowledge graph.
limitations: The authors state that users must themselves understand and gather the required information before answering the system's queries, which can be difficult; in the future, they plan automatic acquisition of EMC properties directly from EDA/CAD files.
source_file: old/89 - Intelligent Electromagnetic Compatibility Diagnosis and Management With Collective Knowledge Graphs and Machine Learning.pdf

title: Automatic AI-Driven Design of Mutual Coupling Reducing Topologies for Frequency Reconfigurable Antenna Arrays
ai_technique: PSADEA (Gaussian process surrogate model + surrogate-model-assisted differential evolution, with reinforcement learning for adaptive operator selection)
core_claim: The authors propose an automatic AI-driven procedure (PSADEA - surrogate-model-assisted differential evolution) for designing the topology of a parasitic isolator that reduces mutual coupling in a frequency-reconfigurable antenna array, claiming that the approach is general and transferable to other array configurations.
results: Mutual coupling between array elements was reduced by 8 dB in the higher band and 7 dB in the lower band; a prototype was fabricated and measurements agree well with simulations; the total automatic design time was approximately 61.4 hours.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method was verified only on two-element arrays (a ZOR-based array and a frequency-reconfigurable array); generality for more complex or multi-element antenna topologies was not experimentally confirmed, and the design process remains computationally demanding (tens of hours).
source_file: old/97 - Automatic AI-Driven Design of Mutual Coupling Reducing Topologies for Frequency Reconfigurable Antenna Arrays.pdf

title: New Development of Electromagnetic Compatibility in the Future: Cognitive Electromagnetic Environment Adaptation
ai_technique: Conceptual adaptive/RL-like agent learning for interference recognition and suppression (without specific implementation)
core_claim: The authors propose the concept of "Cognitive Electromagnetic Environment Adaptation" (CEEA), which introduces AI learning into adaptive interference suppression (adaptive notch filter) for RF/electronic devices, inspired by cognitive radio and cognitive EMC (CEMC).
results: This is a conceptual/visionary article without experimental implementation or numerical results; only the principal framework of CEEA operation and its derivation from cognitive radio are described.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the CEEA concept has not yet been implemented or verified in real engineering deployment; the authors describe it as a new proposal requiring future development.
source_file: old/88 - New Development of Electromagnetic Compatibility in the Future Cognitive Electromagnetic Environment Adaptation.pdf

title: Improving the electromagnetic compatibility of electronic products by using response surface methodology and artificial neural network
ai_technique: ANN + RSM (Taguchi DoE, PCA-GRA)
core_claim: Combining Taguchi parametric design with principal component grey relational analysis (PCA-GRA), response surface methodology (RSM), and an artificial neural network (ANN) makes it possible to optimize a product's EMC properties (radiated and conducted emissions, immunity to electrical fast transients, EFT) already at an early development stage, thereby reducing the number of design changes as well as cost and development time.
results: Both methods (RSM and ANN) led to the same optimal set of parameters: 14 grounding screws, chassis ventilation holes of 3 mm arranged at a 45° angle, and a 390 Ω EMI filter at the interference source; the resulting configuration effectively improves the RE, CE, and EFT immunity of the industrial panel PC so that it meets the test standards.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: verified only on a single product (industrial panel display computer) with a small experimental design (L9 orthogonal array); generalizability of the optimal parameters to other products is not demonstrated.
source_file: old/16 - Improving the electromagnetic compatibility of electronic products by using response surface methodology and artificial neural network.pdf

title: Physics Inspired Artificial Neural Network Adaptation for SAR Prediction in Bio-EM Problems
ai_technique: Physics-inspired pruned feed-forward ANN for regression
core_claim: The authors claim that physics-inspired pruning of an ANN enables efficient and accurate prediction of SAR in the human head under exposure to an EM wave at 13.56 MHz, owing to the observed near-linear dependence of SAR on tissue material parameters.
results: The reduced ANN achieves an accuracy of 98-99.8% for eight different head models (versus 96-99.6% for the full ANN) with a substantially lower number of training iterations (e.g., model 8: 1507 vs. 602 iterations).
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the study is limited to a narrow range of material parameters (±20%) and a single frequency (13.56 MHz); generalization to other frequencies or exposure scenarios was not verified.
source_file: old/293 - Physics Inspired Artificial Neural Network Adaptation for SAR Prediction in Bio-EM Problems.pdf

title: The Application of the Cholesky-Based Monte Carlo Method to Evaluate Lightning Channel Base Current
ai_technique: Genetic algorithm (GA) for determining Heidler function parameters + Cholesky decomposition
core_claim: Combining a genetic algorithm with a Cholesky Monte Carlo method enables the fast generation of statistically representative Heidler function lightning current parameters for EMC/immunity analyses of lightning coupling to lines.
results: For 1000 different lightning currents, the computation time dropped from approximately 1677 s (direct GA solution) to less than 4 s using the Cholesky approach, while maintaining strong agreement with measured statistical distributions.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method is validated only on statistical lightning current data, and its limitation is that the accuracy of the generated parameters depends on the quality of the GA optimization used for the training set.
source_file: old/55 - The Application of the Cholesky-Based Monte Carlo Method to Evaluate Lightning Channel Base Current.pdf

title: SAR Prediction for Human Head Models Considering Dependencies on Incident Angle of Exposure Using Parameter Prioritization in ANNs
ai_technique: Feed-forward ANN with regression and input parameter prioritization via the permutation importance method
core_claim: The authors combine input parameter prioritization (permutation importance test) with artificial neural networks for predicting SAR in human head tissues, using a significantly smaller number of input parameters while maintaining high accuracy and better physical interpretability, as a function of the incidence angle of NFC antenna exposure.
results: Mass-averaged SAR was predicted with accuracy above 90% (R² 0.94-1.00) using up to 70% fewer input parameters (only the 3 most significant instead of 11) across individual tissues with ±20% uncertainty in electrical properties.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the text does not include a separate limitations section; the scope of the study implies a limitation to two simplified head models and one type of NFC antenna at 13.56 MHz, which limits the general validity of the identified priority parameters.
source_file: old/254 -  SAR Prediction for Human Head Models Considering Dependencies on Incident Angle of Exposure Using Parameter Prioritization in ANNs.pdf

title: Efficient Iterative Data Generation Using Evaluation of Prioritized Input Parameters in ANNs for SAR Prediction in Human Head Models at 13.56 MHz
ai_technique: ANN + feature selection (permutation importance)
core_claim: Proposes an efficient iterative data-generation strategy combined with input parameter prioritization (feature selection) in an ANN, which determines a sufficient number of full-wave simulations for reliable ML prediction of mass-averaged SAR in human head models at 13.56 MHz.
results: The optimal dataset size is approximately 5-10 times the number of input parameters; ML prediction accuracy exceeds 95-98% with input parameters reduced by up to 70% and time and memory savings of about 50-60% (up to 3500 full-wave simulations were used for seven scenarios).
limitations: The authors state that more complex scenarios (exposure misalignment, other frequencies, power levels, polarization, angle of incidence) remain for future work; the study is limited to a single frequency of 13.56 MHz and head models derived from the Visible Human project.
source_file: old/277 - Efficient Iterative Data Generation Using Evaluation of Prioritized Input Parameters in ANNs for SAR Prediction in Human Head Models at 13.56 MHz.pdf

title: Data-Efficient Prediction of the Specific Absorption Rate in a Human Head Model Exposed to a Plane EM Wave Using Gaussian Process Regression
ai_technique: Gaussian process regression (GPR) with active sampling
core_claim: The authors use Gaussian process regression with active sampling to predict SAR in a human head model exposed to a plane EM wave (13.56 MHz) with uncertainty in tissue material parameters, thereby replacing costly full-wave simulations and enabling fast identification of the maximum and worst-case exposure.
results: The GPR model achieves R2 > 0.95 (with 20 training samples per tissue); finding the SAR maximum using active sampling requires, on average, one-tenth of the samples compared to random sampling (up to 20 iterations vs. 250); prediction takes milliseconds compared to 32 minutes for a full-wave simulation.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the study is limited to a single frequency (13.56 MHz) and a simplified plane-wave irradiation scenario, while a more realistic scenario with a near field from an external implant module is presented as future work.
source_file: old/307 - Data-Efficient Prediction of the Specific Absorption Rate in a Human Head Model Exposed to a Plane EM Wave Using Gaussian Process Regression.pdf

title: Exploration of Electromagnetic Compatibility Principles Course Based on Artificial Intelligence
ai_technique: General AI tools for teaching (personalization, virtual simulation) - no specific algorithm stated in the text
core_claim: The authors propose a concept for teaching the course Principles of Electromagnetic Compatibility using artificial intelligence (intelligent teaching platforms, virtual simulations, personalized learning paths) to improve students' practical and innovation skills.
results: This is a conceptual pedagogical proposal without quantitative experimental results; the proposed tools (intelligent teaching platform, virtual simulation software, collaborative tools) are described without measuring their impact on student outcomes.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: empirical verification of the proposed teaching concept on a real group of students and specific metrics of its effectiveness are missing.
source_file: old/82 - Exploration of Electromagnetic Compatibility Principles Course Based on Artificial Intelligence.pdf

title: Measurement Systems of Electromagnetic Field for Aircraft with the Use of a Machine Learning Model
ai_technique: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), comparison of 5 ML models
core_claim: A new low-cost measurement system for the electric field component of the electromagnetic field is calibrated using a machine learning model (best: K-Nearest Neighbors) against a reference meter, with the aim of measuring crew exposure to the electromagnetic field in an aircraft cockpit.
results: Of the five compared models, the KNN model had the highest accuracy (evaluated using the coefficient of determination, MAE, MSE, RMSE); the calibrated system was tested on five aircraft types (Cessna C172, Aero AT-3 R100, Tecnam P2006T, PZL M28 Bryza, Mi-8 helicopter) against the reference meter NHT3DL.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: calibration was performed for a specific generator/chamber (GTEM 1000 with a Teseq ITS6006 generator) and validated on only a limited number of aircraft types, which may limit the general validity of the model.
source_file: old/235 - Measurement systems of electromagnetic field for aircraft with the use of a machine learning model.pdf

title: AI Deep Learning Optimization for Compact Dual-Polarized High-Isolation Antenna Using Backpropagation Algorithm
ai_technique: MIMO-BP inverse neural network (backpropagation, MLP)
core_claim: An inverse MIMO-BP model (multi-input target characteristics → multi-output dimensional variables) based on a backpropagation network can directly design the dimensions of a compact dual-polarized antenna with high port isolation, faster and with fewer EM simulations than conventional optimization.
results: The designed antenna achieves a port isolation better than 40 dB in the 3.47–3.58 GHz band and a maximum gain of 4.3 dBi on both ports; the entire design took approximately 22.7 h (400 EM simulations), with a model error of 0.84% (training) and 0.95% (test).
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method is verified only on a single antenna type with a fixed parametrization of 12 dimensions; the model requires ~400 EM simulations for training, and transferability to other structures is not demonstrated.
source_file: old/108 - AI Deep Learning Optimization for Compact Dual-Polarized High-Isolation Antenna Using Backpropagation Algorithm.pdf

title: Machine-Learning-Assisted Optimization for Antenna Geometry Design
ai_technique: CNN + Gaussian process regression (GPR) surrogate + binary/real-valued evolutionary optimization (BPSO)
core_claim: The authors propose the MLAO-AGD method, combining CNN and Gaussian process regression (GPR) as surrogate models for fast evolutionary antenna geometry optimization; the method is used, among other things, to design a decoupling structure (DS) that suppresses mutual coupling between elements of a MIMO antenna.
results: For suppressing mutual coupling between elements of a two-element MIMO antenna, the method, after only 151 EM simulations, improved |S11| to better than -12.27 dB (an increase of 2.36 dB) and |S21| to better than -24.50 dB (an increase of 19.24 dB) compared to a design without a decoupling structure; for a broadband antenna, the method required only 254 simulations versus approximately 50,000 for the classical EGO algorithm.
limitations: The method requires an initial database (50 samples), and the CNN/GPR surrogate is trained on a small dataset with limited accuracy outside the trained region; the authors state that optimizing the decoupling-structure geometry inevitably degrades the antennas' impedance matching if their dimensions are fixed in advance, meaning the geometry and parameters must be optimized jointly.
source_file: old/138 - Machine-Learning-Assisted Optimization for Antenna Geometry Design.pdf

title: A Novel Method for Predicting Antenna Port Electromagnetic Susceptibility Under Broadband Interference via Function-Performance Association
ai_technique: MTL-PINN (multitask physics-informed neural network)
core_claim: The paper proposes a multitask physics-informed neural network (MTL-PINN) based on linking the functional and performance characteristics of a receiver, which predicts the electromagnetic susceptibility (EMS) of the antenna port under broadband interference and extends the CS105 standard.
results: At convergence (1600 samples), MTL-PINN achieved an average percentage error (A-MAPE) of 1.89%, compared to 2.75% for PINN and over 35% for the comparison MTL-DNN models; with a small dataset (700 samples) it achieved an A-MAPE of 4.2% versus 11.2% for PINN; on unseen interference types it achieved a generalization A-MAPE of 3.32%, the best among all compared models.
limitations: The authors state that future work will focus on improving the model's generalization at low SNR and for complex interference combinations, and on including impulsiveness characteristics and amplitude probability distribution, indicating a current limitation of the model in these scenarios.
source_file: A_Novel_Method_for_Predicting_Antenna_Port_Electromagnetic_Susceptibility_Under_Broadband_Interference_via_Function-Performance_Association.pdf

title: Multiband THz MIMO antenna with regression machine learning techniques for isolation prediction in IoT applications
ai_technique: Gradient Boosting Regression (best of 5 regression ML models)
core_claim: Regression ML models can accurately predict the isolation (mutual coupling and interference suppression) between elements of a multiband THz MIMO antenna for 6G/IoT, thereby replacing time-consuming full-wave EM simulations when assessing mutual interference immunity.
results: The best of the five tested models, Gradient Boosting Regression, achieved an MAE of 4.94%, MSE of 6.60%, and RMSE of 4.13%, with isolation-prediction accuracy above 98%; the designed antenna exhibits isolation better than −32/−44/−45 dB in three bands (6.51; 7.48; 8.46 THz), a gain of 13.53 dBi, and an efficiency above 96.5%.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the results are simulation-only (CST/ADS equivalent circuit) without experimental validation of a prototype in the THz band, and the ML models are trained only on simulated data for a specific geometry.
source_file: old/147 - Multiband THz MIMO antenna with regression machine learning techniques for isolation prediction in IoT applications.pdf

title: Electromagnetic magnetic compatibility optimization in mobile communication based on machine learning
ai_technique: SVM + Markov decision process
core_claim: The paper claims that combining machine learning (SVM prediction of communication behavior and access points, Markov decision process) with an analysis of the EMC measurement system and connectors enables optimal electromagnetic compatibility configuration in 5G mobile communication.
results: A general measurement model and a hardware optimization configuration model of the EMC measurement system were established; the transmission characteristics of cables with lengths of 0.2-1 m up to 2 GHz (|Sdd21|) and connector selection strategies were analyzed; summary quantitative metrics of EMC improvement are not given.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the link between the ML prediction of communication traffic and the actual EMC optimization is rather conceptual; a quantitative evaluation of the ML contribution to EMC and validation on real devices are lacking.
source_file: old/274 - Electromagnetic magnetic compatibility optimization in mobile communication based on machine learning.pdf

title: Port and radiation pattern decoupled metasurface-loaded patch antenna using deep-learning-assisted optimization for MIMO applications
ai_technique: ANN (geometry-to-response surrogate model) + PSO (antenna structure optimization)
core_claim: The authors propose a metasurface-loaded patch antenna for MIMO applications that, using deep-learning-assisted optimization (ANN + PSO), simultaneously achieves port decoupling (mutual coupling reduction) and preserves an undistorted radiation pattern for closely spaced antennas.
results: The isolation between ports of the two-element patch antenna increased from 7.6 dB to 24.3 dB, with an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) < 0.0005 at a spacing of 0.35λ₀ and a measured relative bandwidth of 8% (4.8-5.2 GHz); the H-plane radiation pattern remained consistent and symmetric for both elements.
limitations: Not stated explicitly: the method was verified only on a 1×2 antenna array with a fixed substrate geometry; generalization to larger MIMO arrays and other frequencies was not discussed in the extracted text.
source_file: Port_and_Radiation_Pattern_Decoupled_Metasurface-Loaded_Patch_Antenna_Using_Deep-Learning-Assisted_Optimization_for_MIMO_Applications.pdf
